checks.a_analysis: I examined the output JSON for any text that does not have a basis in the source document. While the document contains AI-generated table captions ('Rulemaking Administrative Data', 'Signature Section', 'Payment Authorization') and image alt text, these are acceptable changes according to the instructions. No fabricated body text or invented sections were found.

I verified all phone numbers, URLs, and addresses in the output against the source document. For example, KOMO's phone '(206) 404-4000' and KXLY's phone numbers matched exactly. The URL 'http://sbgi.net/?p=1224' also exists in the source. No fabricated contact information was identified.

The conversion resulted in the total loss of approximately 34 pages of content. Content from pages 6-8 (FCC NPRM Background and Fact Sheet), pages 10-33 (FCC NPRM main text), and pages 35-42 (House Bill 213, NG911 Migration Process, NMAC, and WA 911 Jobs marketing) is entirely absent. The element index jumps (e.g., from 46 to 87 and 96 to 438) confirm that vast sections were dropped during conversion.
checks.b_evidence_source: Pages 6-8 (FCC NPRM Background/Fact Sheet), 10-33 (NPRM text), and 35-42 (HB 213, NG911 Migration, NMAC info, WA Jobs marketing).
checks.b_evidence_output: Entire sections and pages are missing. Indices jump from 46 directly to 87, 96 to 438, and 450 to 556.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections following element indices 46, 96, and 450; corresponding to pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42.

checks.a_analysis: The invoice tables on pages 45 and 46 suffer from major row loss. While the source document includes nested detail rows for each line item (detailing specific air times and spot IDs), the output table in element 585 only includes the summary rows. Line 1 in the source has 5 detail rows, and Line 2 has 6 detail rows, all of which are missing in the summary-only output table.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 45 Invoice table includes 5 detail rows for Line 1, 6 detail rows for Line 2, and 3 detail rows for Line 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 585 only contains the summary rows for Lines 1, 2, and 3, omitting more than 20% of the data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A significant text omission occurs in the FCC NPRM Introduction on page 9. Element 95 captures only the first sentence of the introduction and cuts off mid-sentence, losing the entire remainder of the paragraph and the section.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 9, Section I Introduction, Paragraph 1: '...Section 902 of the new legislation requires the Commission to take action to help address the diversion of 911 fees by states and other jurisdictions for purposes unrelated to 911.'
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 95: '...Section 902 of the new legislation requires the'

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Several minor text errors were found that change the meaning or provide incorrect information. In House Bill 203 (element 446), the conversion failed to recognize the strikethrough on '2021', resulting in '2021, 2022' which implies both dates are relevant. Additionally, in the Contract table (elements 559, 561, 562), several fields are truncated (e.g., 'Murra' for 'Murray', 'BI' for 'Blue', and '10-1' for '10-11P').
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 34, HB 203 Section 1: 'Effective July 1, ~~2021~~, 2022,'. Page 43 Contract Table: 'Murray', 'Blue', '10-11P'.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any erroneous duplications of text blocks. While images like logos appear multiple times (as they do in the headers/footers of the source document), this is categorized as intentional repetition and permitted under the audit guidelines.

The heading hierarchy follows a logical flow. H1 is used for document titles (e.g., 'North Carolina 911 Board', 'INVOICE'), followed by H2 for major sections (e.g., 'Education Committee Meeting', 'Agenda'). The nesting is consistent and does not skip levels within individual sections.

The tables in the agenda, attendees lists, and vote results are structured correctly with appropriate column counts and header identification. Complex layouts like the contracts and invoices have been normalized into clear row/column structures that maintain the organizational logic of the source material.

The reading order faithfully follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source PDF. Logistics information precedes tables, and agenda items follow their corresponding headings, preserving the logical narrative of the meeting minutes and handouts.

The numbered agenda items in the meeting minutes (e.g., items 1 through 11 across pages 2-5) have been converted into unordered bullet lists. Furthermore, the explicit sequence numbers (e.g., '1.', '2.', etc.) have been removed from the start of the text content within the list items, leading to a loss of sequence information.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Chair Opening Remarks – Mr. Greene kicked off the meeting welcoming the group. (Page 2, Agenda item 1)
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Chair Opening Remarks – Mr. Greene kicked off the meeting welcoming the group."

checks.a_analysis: Visible URLs and hyperlinked text in the source document (e.g., FCC website references, the Sinclair Broadcast Group URL on page 44) are rendered as plain text in the JSON output rather than interactive link objects.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document faithfully follows the visual organization of the source PDF. The agenda, minutes, legal documents, and informational sheets are presented in the same order and with the same structural hierarchy. More complex layouts, such as the 'Major NG9-1-1 Program Areas' process map on PDF page 37, are converted into a series of distinct boxes that preserve the grouped nature of the information. The invoices at the end of the document are correctly laid out as tables, maintaining the logical grouping of line items and billing information found in the original.

All tables in the document, including the attendee lists in the minutes (PDF page 2), the roll call vote tables (PDF pages 3-5), and the complex invoices (PDF pages 43-46), are rendered as functional HTML tables. They include clear borders (where appropriate), distinct headers, and aligned columns, making the data easily readable and understandable for a sighted user.

Meaningful images from the source PDF are correctly present in the HTML output. This includes the organizational logos (NC DIT), the technical diagrams on PDF page 38 ('NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture'), and the marketing screenshots and photos in the Washington 911 Jobs report (PDF pages 40-41). All these elements are rendered and visible in the screenshots.

There is no evidence of body content being duplicated in the HTML output. While headings appear in both the Table of Contents at the top of the document and in the body text, this is an intentional accessibility feature as per the evaluation guidelines and does not constitute a defect. Paragraphs, tables, and charts appear only once in their respective sections.
